title: Idempotency as the basis for safe retries
description: Why retries and backfills are only safe when a task's effect for a given date is the same no matter how many times it runs.
body: Use when reasoning about whether automatic retries are safe to enable. A task is idempotent if running it twice for the same execution date leaves the same result; retries and backfills depend on this because the engine may run a task more than once. Do not enable retries on a task that appends or sends a non-deduplicated side effect — duplicates result. Actions: design writes as overwrite/merge per date partition and make external effects deduplicated by a date key. Validation signals include identical output across reruns and a side effect that is not duplicated on retry. Risk boundary: retrying a non-idempotent task multiplies its effect; if idempotency cannot be guaranteed, escalate to disable auto-retry and handle failures manually.
